sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: KVC Westerlo are in poor form, winless in their last 7 matches with only 2 draws and multiple losses, struggling to convert chances (e.g., 6 big chances missed vs. KAA Gent). RSC Anderlecht, in contrast, are on a strong run with 4 wins and 1 draw in their last 5 matches, showcasing resilience with late goals (e.g., 90+2 winner vs. RAAL La Louvière). Anderlecht’s ability to score early and defend well gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results were split: Anderlecht crushed Westerlo 5-2 in July 2025, dominating with 5 big chances, while Westerlo secured a 2-0 home win in March 2025 with early goals. Historically, matches between these sides often see over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in 5 of the last 6 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Westerlo (13th, 17 points) have scored 21 goals but conceded 26, with a poor defensive record (13th in least goals conceded). Anderlecht (4th, 31 points) boast a balanced attack (23 goals) and solid defense (13 conceded), often controlling games with high final third entries. Westerlo’s knack for creating big chances (3rd in league) could pose a threat if converted.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Westerlo are missing key players like Arthur Piedfort, impacting midfield creativity, while Anderlecht lack Thorgan Hazard, reducing attacking flair. Anderlecht’s streak of scoring first in 5 of their last 6 games, combined with Westerlo’s tendency to concede early, could dictate the match tempo. Referee Lawrence Visser’s high yellow card average (4.7 per game) suggests a physical battle.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Based on current form, historical data, and Sofascore’s probabilities (home win 29%, draw 29%, away win 42%), RSC Anderlecht are favored to win. Westerlo’s home advantage at Het Kuipje might not be enough against Anderlecht’s momentum and superior league standing. Expect a competitive match with goals likely on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, KVC Westerlo holds a commanding 3-0 lead over RSC Anderlecht at Het Kuipje. Westerlo has been clinical in front of goal, converting their chances with precision. The home side scored early through Arthur Piedfort and added two more via Allahyar Sayyadmanesh, including a penalty just before the break. Anderlecht, despite dominating possession, has struggled to create meaningful opportunities and has been unable to breach Westerlo's defense effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Westerlo's performance in the first half was marked by efficiency and defensive solidity. Despite having less possession, they made the most of their entries into the final third and penalty area, capitalizing on key moments. Their expected goals reflect a strong attacking output compared to Anderlecht's underwhelming numbers. On the other hand, Anderlecht controlled the ball for much of the half but failed to translate their dominance into dangerous chances. Their lack of big chances created and minimal shots on target highlight a disconnect in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Westerlo, Allahyar Sayyadmanesh has been the standout performer, netting twice and posing a constant threat. Arthur Piedfort also made an early impact with a well-taken goal, while players like Dogucan Haspolat have been instrumental in creating opportunities from midfield. On Anderlecht's side, no player has truly stepped up in the attacking third, though Mario Stroeykens showed some intent with a shot on target. Yari Verschaeren has tried to influence play with key passes, but overall, the away side lacks a cutting edge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Westerlo started brightly with an early burst that led to their opening goal, maintaining strong spells of pressure throughout the half. Their attacking intent was particularly evident in the closing minutes, culminating in two more goals. Anderlecht had extended periods of momentum, especially in the middle of the half, with several sustained attacks, but they couldn't turn these phases into concrete chances. The momentum shifted back to Westerlo late in the half as they capitalized on their opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Westerlo will likely aim to protect their lead while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. Given their clinical nature in the first half, they could add to their tally if Anderlecht pushes forward. Anderlecht, trailing by three, will need to take risks and commit more players forward to salvage something from this match. However, their lack of potency in attack so far suggests a comeback might be difficult unless they drastically improve their finishing or create better chances.
